title: Springfield Up

jokes.text: These children ran the gamut of society: rich and poor, black and white, 'he'll grow into his looks' and 'forever hideous.'
jokes.setup: Documentary narrator lists societal categories of children
jokes.punchline: Shifts from standard demographics to brutally honest appearance assessments
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic misdirection with perfect escalation from mundane to cruel. Classic Simpsons dark humor disguised as documentary pretension.

jokes.text: My dad was a circus freak, but my mom don't remember which one. I like to think it was a little bit of all of them.
jokes.setup: Child explains father's circus background
jokes.punchline: Innocent acceptance of mother's promiscuity with multiple circus freaks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that combines child innocence with adult implications. The phrase 'all of them' is perfectly delivered for maximum impact.

jokes.text: dreams dissolve like a muffin in the rain
jokes.setup: Documentary narrator attempts poetic metaphor
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and unappetizing comparison
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor mocking documentary pretension. The muffin imagery is unexpectedly grotesque and memorable.

jokes.text: Will he grow up to work in a robot factory? Will she be the receptionist for a robot businessman? And will he be a crusader for robo-sexual rights? I say, 'yes,' 'no,' and 'yes.'
jokes.setup: Narrator poses three future predictions
jokes.punchline: Escalates from mundane robot jobs to robo-sexual rights activism
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy with perfect rule-of-three structure. 'Robo-sexual rights' is brilliantly absurd and ahead of its time.

jokes.text: My cake will be a picture of a dinosaur and all the guests will say, 'Why, Lenny, that's a fine cake!' Then, 'Yes,' I'll say.
jokes.setup: Child Lenny describes his dream birthday party
jokes.punchline: Mundane cake conversation with formal dialogue that sounds rehearsed
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Lenny's simple nature even as a child. The formal dialogue structure makes it absurdly endearing.

jokes.text: I decided not to waste any more film on him after that.
jokes.setup: Lenny's innocent birthday cake story
jokes.punchline: Documentary filmmaker's brutal dismissal of boring subject
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy that establishes the documentary filmmaker's character while getting a laugh at Lenny's expense.

jokes.text: I wish for world peace. I wish for world war. Oh, yeah, that would be cooler.
jokes.setup: Child makes noble wish for world peace
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses to wish for world war because it's 'cooler'
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark comedy about child psychology. The word 'cooler' perfectly captures juvenile reasoning about global catastrophe.

jokes.text: I'll own a football team and a basketball team and I'll make them play baseball!
jokes.setup: Young Homer declares he'll be rich and own sports teams
jokes.punchline: Illogical plan to make different sport teams play baseball
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - grandiose ambition combined with complete lack of understanding. Shows his character from childhood.

jokes.text: It hurts! It hurts so bad! Please, just put one in my brain! Okay. Uh, bang. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Child Wiggum playing cops and robbers
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic death scene with polite execution request
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy mixing child's play with adult themes. The polite 'thank you' after execution is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Hey, detention bird! Thanks for not running in the hall. Go, Wildcats.
jokes.setup: Hall monitor Wiggum addressing rule-breaking student
jokes.punchline: Thanks them for NOT running then randomly cheers for school team
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Wiggum's confusion and misplaced enthusiasm. 'Detention bird' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: What did I tell you about pointsy-towardsies?!
jokes.setup: Police academy training scene
jokes.punchline: Made-up childish term for pointing guns
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that infantilizes police training. 'Pointsy-towardsies' is memorably silly.

jokes.text: You seem a little cranky, Commandant. A back rub'll set you right. There we go. Don't be afraid to dig in there good!
jokes.setup: Wiggum addresses angry police instructor
jokes.punchline: Offers and gives unsolicited back massage with intimate coaching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum character comedy - inappropriate but well-meaning. The physical intimacy makes it cringily funny.

jokes.text: If I was Chief, I could give you 8:30 every day.
jokes.setup: Wiggum giving back massage to superior
jokes.punchline: Offers daily massage appointments if promoted to chief
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Wiggum's misguided approach to career advancement through massage therapy.

jokes.text: I'm not making fun of you. I'm making fun of your pants.
jokes.setup: Wiggum complains about being made fun of
jokes.punchline: Technical distinction between mocking person vs. clothing
jokes.explanation: Decent deadpan comedy with logical but pedantic distinction. Shows filmmaker's character.

jokes.text: Well, they're a little, uh... They-they, uh... Oh, they're perfect.
jokes.setup: Wiggum tries to insult filmmaker's pants in return
jokes.punchline: Can't find anything wrong and admits they're perfect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's inability to be mean. The stammering builds to honest admission.

jokes.text: None. You never had any. I'm not a genie. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Young Homer asks how many wishes he has left
jokes.punchline: Filmmaker crushes magical thinking with reality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on childhood wish-granting fantasies. Homer's 'D'oh!' shows early character development.

jokes.text: Don't look at me. Look at the camera. Got it. I said, look at the camera. No problem. Now you're looking at a mud puddle. That's your hand. That's the production accountant. That's your other hand. Do you even know what a camera is? Well, of cour... No.
jokes.setup: Filmmaker tries to direct Homer to look at camera
jokes.punchline: Homer looks at everything except camera, doesn't know what camera is
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy showing Homer's obliviousness. Perfect character integration with great build-up to confession of ignorance.

jokes.text: I'm gonna put this squeaky toy on top of the camera. Squeaky! Squeaky! Squeaky, squeaky! Squeaky, squeaky, squeaky!
jokes.setup: Filmmaker desperate to get Homer's attention on camera
jokes.punchline: Resorts to dog training methods with squeaky toy
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that reduces Homer to pet-level intelligence. The repetitive squeaking adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Manure for sale! Get your manure! Loamiest in town!
jokes.setup: Adult Homer's business ventures
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically hawking manure with quality claims
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's entrepreneurial delusions. 'Loamiest' is a nice specific detail.

jokes.text: Um, I know Super Clean cleans, but does it scrub?
jokes.setup: Homer's infomercial question-asking job
jokes.punchline: Asks obvious question about cleaning product
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of infomercial tactics and Homer's simple-minded questioning style.

jokes.text: The album cover's gonna be a naked girl with stars for boobs.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his garage band
jokes.punchline: Juvenile album cover concept
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer immaturity joke. Fits character but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: And I do open-casket caricatures. Did he have any hobbies? Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Homer lists his various jobs
jokes.punchline: Draws caricatures at funerals, gets defensive about appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy concept. 'Open-casket caricatures' is hilariously inappropriate and perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: But Homer had found a peanut of hope in his Cracker Jack box of despair.
jokes.setup: Documentary narration about Homer's life
jokes.punchline: Overwrought metaphor mixing hope/despair with snack food
jokes.explanation: Good satirical documentary-speak. The Cracker Jack metaphor is absurdly specific and memorable.

jokes.text: He can chug a beer and pitch woo at the same time. The light from your eyes could guide a ship at sea.
jokes.setup: Marge describing Homer's romantic abilities
jokes.punchline: Combines drinking prowess with cheesy romantic line
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's multitasking romance skills and overwrought poetry.

jokes.text: Kids? No way! You'll never see a couple of rugrats tying me down!
jokes.setup: Young Homer declares he'll never have children
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration that viewers know is false
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony since viewers know Homer has kids. Sets up documentary editing joke.

jokes.text: You better not put this shot after the one where I said I won't have kids. That would be a devastating edit.
jokes.setup: Homer aware he's being filmed with children present
jokes.punchline: Correctly predicts the obvious documentary editing choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy about documentary filmmaking conventions. Homer's media awareness is surprisingly sharp.

jokes.text: You're not English Breakfast, but you're not Lapsang Soochong either.
jokes.setup: Filmmaker offers backhanded compliment to Homer
jokes.punchline: Compares life quality to tea varieties
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific metaphor showing documentary filmmaker's pretentious British nature through tea comparison.

jokes.text: Check with me in eight years, Dr. Who. I'll be kickin' your ass with a solid gold boot!
jokes.setup: Homer responds to filmmaker's tea comparison insult
jokes.punchline: Threatens violence with specifically valuable footwear
jokes.explanation: Good Homer bluster with specific absurd detail. 'Solid gold boot' is memorably excessive.

jokes.text: Why do you sound so shocked? This is our fifth take.
jokes.setup: Filmmaker acts surprised at Homer's success
jokes.punchline: Breaks fourth wall about filming multiple takes
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy that breaks the documentary illusion perfectly. Economy is superb - maximum impact in minimal words.

jokes.text: That's why rich kids turn out so well.
jokes.setup: Homer can buy his kids whatever they want
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about spoiled rich children
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic observation about wealth and parenting. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Mine has a cup holder. Bart, that's a blowhole. You're a blowhole!
jokes.setup: Bart claims his item has a cup holder
jokes.punchline: Homer corrects it's a blowhole, Bart turns it into insult
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Bart's defensive nature. The blowhole/insult connection is childishly logical.

jokes.text: Can you edit that? I don't want to say 'chest' in a movie.
jokes.setup: Marge says her chest might burst with pride
jokes.punchline: Immediately regrets using the word 'chest'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge prudishness. Her discomfort with the word 'chest' is endearingly innocent.

jokes.text: We pay him in limes That's right, limes.
jokes.setup: Burns explains Homer's compensation
jokes.punchline: Proudly confirms they pay employee in citrus fruit
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comedy. The proud confirmation 'That's right, limes' makes it even more ridiculous.

jokes.text: You see, Declan, I made my millions with a simple invention: the Condiment Pen!
jokes.setup: Homer reveals his path to wealth
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous invention combining pen and condiments
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist invention that's just plausible enough to be funny. Very Homer-esque solution.

jokes.text: I got the idea from the regular pen.
jokes.setup: Homer explains his condiment pen invention
jokes.punchline: Obviously simple inspiration source
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obvious statement. Shows Homer's simple thought process.

jokes.text: You can put horseradish on your dead mother for all I care.
jokes.setup: Asked if condiment pen works on fish and chips
jokes.punchline: Grotesque escalation to desecrating corpses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation that's shockingly specific and memorable. The casual tone makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Barely Literate, smells Like Feet
jokes.setup: Documentary title card for character segment
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest character description
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy in character description. The combination of intellectual and hygiene failings is perfectly cutting.

jokes.text: Ah, bullies approaching. Activate cloaking device.
jokes.setup: Young Frink sees bullies coming
jokes.punchline: Uses scientific terminology for hiding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink character comedy - applying scientific concepts to childhood problems. Shows his nature from early age.

jokes.text: I've spent my whole life in the lab and never talked to a girl. That's why I built this time machine.
jokes.setup: Frink explains his life's regret about women
jokes.punchline: Builds time machine to fix social problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Frink's tendency to solve personal problems with over-engineered solutions.

jokes.text: one where I'll meet a female woman of the girl-u-lar variety
jokes.setup: Frink explains his goal for career change
jokes.punchline: Overly scientific/awkward description of women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frink-speak that shows his complete social awkwardness. 'Girl-u-lar variety' is perfectly quotable nonsense.

jokes.text: That was... Hey, I'm still going, but...
jokes.setup: Frink tries to deliver important message to past self
jokes.punchline: Time machine continues moving, making conversation impossible
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing the impracticality of Frink's invention. Physical comedy undermines his scientific achievement.

jokes.text: Your Honor, I'd like to request a continuance so I can go deliver a baby. I love babies.
jokes.setup: Eleanor as lawyer-doctor in court
jokes.punchline: Casual request to leave trial for medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Decent premise of dual career conflict but execution is straightforward

jokes.text: So, sometimes-- don't shoot me-- I have a glass of wine with Buster here. He's a real comfort.
jokes.setup: Successful woman admits to drinking
jokes.punchline: Defensive about moderate wine consumption with cat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing professional woman's isolation and defensive nature about normal behavior.

jokes.text: Aw, crap, my first kiss. You could do a lot worse, my friend
jokes.setup: Photographer accidentally captures Marge's first kiss
jokes.punchline: Marge's resigned acceptance and awkward reassurance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's practical nature even in romantic situations. Self-deprecating and endearing.

jokes.text: I got a picture of you tattooed on my chest. They had a sale on skulls.
jokes.setup: Homer got Marge's picture tattooed
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was changed to skull due to sale price
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - romantic gesture ruined by cheapness. Perfect character integration showing his misguided priorities.

jokes.text: so he has time to work on his rock music, his erotic etchings, his Play-Doh Theater...
jokes.setup: Marge explains why she needs full-time work
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly ridiculous artistic pursuits
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from reasonable to absurd. 'Play-Doh Theater' is perfectly childish for Homer's artistic pretensions.

jokes.text: As shocks go, this one's a real zaparoo.
jokes.setup: Marge describes being shocked by sudden wealth
jokes.punchline: Made-up intensifier word 'zaparoo'
jokes.explanation: Decent made-up word humor showing Marge's wholesome vocabulary. 'Zaparoo' is memorable if not brilliant.

jokes.text: This is Eduardo, my pool boy. He thinks he's an angry, rich man. I am an angry, rich man. That's the pool chemicals talking.
jokes.setup: Homer introduces 'pool boy' Eduardo
jokes.punchline: Eduardo is actually Burns, dismissed as chemical-addled
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal comedy. Homer's obliviousness to Burns' actual identity is perfectly executed with great structure.

jokes.text: You can have the boy. Just don't beat him. You can beat him. Just don't leave marks.
jokes.setup: Homer begging Burns not to hurt his son
jokes.punchline: Immediately compromises on child abuse terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing Homer's quick moral compromises. The escalation structure is perfect.

jokes.text: Using the suspension spring above the pendulum leader, hang the pendulum on the pendulum guide...
jokes.setup: Burns trapped in grandfather clock
jokes.punchline: Only has boring clock instructions to read
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist torture - Burns forced to read technical manual. The specific jargon adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Please get me out of here. It's 11:59:59.
jokes.setup: Smithers trapped in grandfather clock
jokes.punchline: About to be struck by clock mechanism at midnight
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with precise timing element. The countdown creates genuine tension and humor.

jokes.text: Shadow and Winston have been fighting a lot. Put Winston in the front. Winston doesn't like the front.
jokes.setup: Burns wants to release the hounds
jokes.punchline: Detailed discussion of dogs' seating preferences and conflicts
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing excessive concern for attack dogs' personal preferences. Humanizes the hounds absurdly.

jokes.text: 'Why lie?' and 'Lies... why?'
jokes.setup: Filmmaker has two questions for Homer
jokes.punchline: Two versions of essentially the same question
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing documentary filmmaker's pretentious nature. The redundancy is the joke.

jokes.text: Did you ever try that new shampoo? Nope, never did. Want to watch me pay my cable bill? I got checks with butterflies on them. - I am interesting. - No, you're not.
jokes.setup: Lenny tries to prove he's interesting with mundane activities
jokes.punchline: Filmmaker's brutal honesty about Lenny's dullness
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier Lenny segment. Perfect character consistency and brutal honesty from filmmaker.

jokes.text: I am interesting. No, you're not.
jokes.setup: Lenny insisting he's interesting
jokes.punchline: Filmmaker's blunt rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective cringe moment with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Same job, same house, same dirty joke book.
jokes.setup: Homer explains his disappointment with life
jokes.punchline: Includes joke book among life's major constants
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Homer's priorities. The dirty joke book as life constant is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Strong words from a dumpy man.
jokes.setup: Homer's emotional confession about feeling inadequate
jokes.punchline: Filmmaker's callous dismissal
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing filmmaker's lack of empathy

jokes.text: Wait just a cotton-swabbing minute!
jokes.setup: Marge getting angry at filmmaker
jokes.punchline: Wholesome substitute for profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character moment. 'Cotton-swabbing' as a clean expletive substitute is endearingly innocent.

jokes.text: What's the matter, Declan? Did a cop give you a ticket for talking like a fruit?
jokes.setup: Declan seems upset
jokes.punchline: Crude joke about his accent/nationality
jokes.explanation: Standard Barney crudeness targeting foreign accent. Fits character but relies on cheap stereotype humor.

jokes.text: I actually feel affection for these knuckle-dragging sub-monkeys.
jokes.setup: Filmmaker experiencing character growth
jokes.punchline: Expressing affection with insulting terms
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing elitist learning to care despite prejudices

jokes.text: - That's an ad for shoe inserts. - What the....? I've been writing creepy letters to that?
jokes.setup: Barney thinks he's watching scrambled porn
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been aroused by shoe insert commercial
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal comedy with perfect character integration. The 'creepy letters' detail makes it even more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: An action movie where I play the Pope who kills the president? No, that's a terrible idea. Yeah, I know. It's stupid. I think it could work, though. I've even got a title: Pontiff No Return. I came up with it, but I don't really get it.
jokes.setup: Barney suggests movie idea
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun title that he doesn't understand himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy. 'Pontiff No Return' is a terrible pun that Barney created but doesn't understand - perfect character moment.

jokes.text: O, spirit! Are you gonna show me my future? My snow-flecked grave, mourned by no man?
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he's about to see his future
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reference to A Christmas Carol
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference showing Homer's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: O, spirit! Are you gonna show me my future? My snow-flecked grave, mourned by no man? Well, it's not too late for me to change! You there, boy! Buy me a Christmas goose! The biggest one in the shop! And then what?
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's in A Christmas Carol scenario
jokes.punchline: Runs out of Christmas Carol knowledge after goose purchase
jokes.explanation: Good literary parody showing Homer's surface-level cultural knowledge. 'And then what?' reveals his confusion perfectly.

jokes.text: He picked the perfect time to lock in his mortgage.
jokes.setup: Compilation of Homer praise from townspeople
jokes.punchline: Ned's practical financial observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - practical and neighborly observation about Homer's financial timing.

jokes.text: Homer gave me a kidney. It wasn't his, I didn't need it, and it came postage due, but still, a lovely gesture.
jokes.setup: Moe describes Homer's generosity
jokes.punchline: Three-part revelation that ruins the generous gesture
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy with perfect rule-of-three structure. Each revelation makes Homer's 'generosity' more absurd. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: At bedtime, he tucks me in tighter than anyone else could. And you know what? No monsters have gotten me yet.
jokes.setup: Lisa describes Homer's bedtime routine
jokes.punchline: Credits lack of monster attacks to tight tucking
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with child logic. Lisa's scientific approach to monster prevention is endearing.

jokes.text: hanging out with my family, drinking with my friends, making friends with my family and hanging with my drinking.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes what he really wants in life
jokes.punchline: Gets confused in parallel structure, mentions drinking twice
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's priorities and verbal confusion. The drinking repetition is perfectly Homer.

tmdb_overview: A documentary filmmaker who interviewed a group of Springfield schoolchildren 32 years ago has followed up at eight-year intervals to film how their lives have unfolded.
